bullets.text: Aftershocks continue to fray nerves in Venezuela days after twin earthquakes devastated areas and left many people to fend for themselves, with residents using basic tools to search for survivors.

The whereabouts of nearly 300 people who have tested positive for Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo are unknown, raising fears of widespread community transmission and a high death toll by September. France has also confirmed its first Ebola case in a doctor who had worked in the DRC.

Pakistani strikes have reportedly killed at least 28 civilians in Afghanistan, according to the UN. Pakistan claims it was targeting militants, but the Taliban government has condemned the action as an "atrocity."
Jo Clay has been elected as the new leader of the ACT Greens, with Andrew Braddock as deputy, marking a new leadership era after Shane Rattenbury's 14-year tenure.
New changes in NSW effective July 1 will bring relief to motorists, public transport users, and wage earners.
A British midwife who moved to Australia to address staff shortages is advocating for legal changes after being subjected to a $70,000 foreign investment tax.

The Supreme Court has blocked former President Trump from firing a Federal Reserve governor, upholding the central bank's independence in one decision while also allowing a president to fire top regulators in a separate ruling.
Comcast plans to spin off its broadcast and studio businesses, NBCUniversal and Sky, as the media industry grapples with retaining audiences.
China is cracking down on rule-bending offshore investments, aiming to direct mainland investors towards its own technological advancements rather than those in America.
